title: The cost of retained earnings for Duchess Corporation equals the required return on equity. Recall that we calculated th…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: The cost of retained earnings for Duchess Corporation equals the required return on equity. Recall that we calculated the required return using two methods. With the constant-growth model, we estimated the required return on equity to be 13% (before accounting for flotation costs and underpricing), and with the CAPM, the required return on equity was 12%. Thus, the cost for Duchess Corporation to finance investments through retained earnings, rr, falls somewhere in the range of 12.0% to 13.0%. Both estimates are lower than the cost of a new issue of common stock because by using retained earnings the firm avoids the additional costs associated with issuing new equity.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Overall, the cost of retained earnings for Duchess Corporation is a relatively cheap source of financing compared to issuing new equity. It is important for the company to consider this cost when deciding whether to use retained earnings for investments or to seek external financing options. The range of rr between 12.0% and 13.0% provides the company with a good estimate of the cost of using retained earnings for financing.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Bridal Shoppe sells wedding dresses.  The cost of each dress is comprised of the following:  Selling price of $1,000 and variable (flexible) costs of $400.  Total fixed (capacity-related) costs for Bridal Shoppe are $90,000.

A.	What is the contribution margin per dress?

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
The contribution margin is the difference between the selling price and the variable cost, which is:

Contribution margin per dress = Selling price - Variable cost
Contribution margin per dress = $1,000 - $400
Contribution margin per dress = $<<1000-400=600>>600

Therefore, the contribution margin per wedding dress is $600.
